name: Goodwin, Mary Kate
description: As an English major, Mary Kate has been excellent; as an athlete, she has been an outstanding leader; as a student teacher of English and gym, she shows the splendid give-and-take spirit. She is a girl with a vision and realizes that her "reach should exceed her grasp."

name: Wells, Azoline
motto: "A sunny disposition is the very soul of success."
description: Truth and sincerity are the essence of her nature. Modest, winsome, gay and fair. She has that frank sort of friendliness that warms the heart and wins love in every acquaintance.

name: Campbell, Eddie Marie
motto: "A brow of beauty and earnest thought, A form of womanly grace."
description: Eddie's magnetic personality, her purity of soul, her musical talent, have made for her a host of friends. S. T. C. is proud of you, Eddie.

name: Johnson, Verner E.
motto: "Smiles are his talents, too, But we readily admit that his noblest virtue Is a well-formed character, Steadfast and true."
description: Though "Jew" resembles not the Abraham who "went down to Amsterdam," he came to S. T. C. and did just as well—possibly better.

name: Guy, Lucile
motto: "At mathematics she is a star, With her there are none on a par— She has a fine brain, And again In looks she is also a star."

name: Manning, J. E.
description: Ed's happy-go-lucky, devil-may-care manner has made him known to everybody. No matter whether we win or whether we lose, come what may, this "smiling pitcher" pitches on. He brings to you the real optimistic spirit of his homeland, the Delta.

name: Hopkins, Nellie Amelia
description: Nellie is tall and striking, with the dignity of the seventies and the modern charm of the thirties. With all this nature lavished upon her, she has the rare combination of grace, sincerity and friendliness to help her win her way into the hearts of her many schoolmates.

name: Thomas, Vernie
description: Quiet, bashful and unassuming, but always willing to work. Who would dare predict failure for a student possessing these qualities? With her inherent ability and pleasant disposition, Vernie will succeed at whatever task she undertakes.

name: Mcmahan, Homer
description: Carefree, confident and ambitious is Homer. His genial disposition, polished manners and originality in thought and conversation have won for him hundreds of friends. "Mac" is a real football star. A surer tackler never lived. If the best in him cannot bring success, surely the worst will not bring failure.

name: Ruble, Ernestine Virginia
motto: "A good, true friend and jolly pal, Whimsical, witty and wise; A lovable, playful, active girl, Makes good at all she tries."
description: Ernestine is a happy, jubilant being, always looking on the bright side of life, and finding a sunny fun in every situation.

name: Walters, Beatrice Estelle
motto: "Her price is above rubies."
clubs: World Fellowship Committee of Y. W. C. A., '28
description: One might judge that Beatrice determined to imitate Solomon's ideal woman, but, on second thought, her character is too genuine to be an imitation. She just grew that way.

name: Grayson, Sam A.
description: Though modest, "Gentleman" has been written on his unembarrassed, brave character.

name: Hensarling, M. B.
description: "Hens" is a jolly, good fellow. He has been with us four years (seems like ten). He has taken part in most everything on the campus, except the girls. Go ahead, "Hens"—we are for you.

name: Overstreet, Alice
motto: "Be loving, and you will never lack for love."
description: "Little Miss Overstreet"—we say little because of her size—is one of our star students, and her record is one of which we are justly proud. The word "lovable" characterizes the sweet seriousness of this little maiden. Alice's outstanding trait is complete unselfishness.

name: Spinks, Viva O.
description: "Spinksie" is quiet and aloof from the masses, yet underneath her charming modesty we find integrity, sincerity, loyalty, thoroughness and patience—attributes of a priceless friend.

name: Hill, B. L.
motto: "Man is his own star, and the soul that can render an honest and a perfect man commands all light, all influence, all fate."
description: "B. L." is of the sort we like to have around us, in the dormitory, on the campus—anywhere. We love him because of his great big friendship, which, after all, is the greatest of all human traits. Hill is a fine chap.

name: Ethridge, Amy Lee
description: With her Christian ideals and her steadfastness in all she undertakes, Amy Lee has won the admiration of all the students and instructors who know her.

name: Harvison, Mrs. W. A.
description: Only one born of courage, patience and determination will succeed as Mrs. Harvison has. Her student life has been quiet and unassuming, but this reticence did not hide her kindness, responsiveness and strength of character. Her generous nature, noble deeds and high ideals reveal something of her worth.

name: Harrell, Frances Katharine
motto: "Let others find out your importance."
description: Frances is an all "A" student and is popular with both faculty and student body. If smiles would bring riches she would be immensely wealthy, but they bring what we so love to see—the sparkle in her eyes and dimples in her cheeks. This little lass of twenty-one summers has already demonstrated her success as a teacher.

name: Williams, Lela Grace
motto: "Rose leaves, when rose is dead, Are heaped for the beloved's bed; And so thy thoughts, when thou art gone, Love itself shall slumber on."

name: Craft, A. E.
description: Popularity often causes conceit. Not so with Craft; his friendliness, his disposition, and above all, his attitude, causes him to succeed. In summary, let's call him "a true representative of the spirit of S. T. C."

name: Hammerlin, Sara Virginia
description: Beauty and brains make an unusual combination. Sara has both, so here's our congratulations! When called upon she's always right there ready to work and do her share. In other words, she's a typical girl. It's my prophecy she'll make her mark in this world.

name: Hawthorne, Myrtis
description: Myrtis has characteristics that automatically bring success. As a student she has been an efficient, sincere worker. Her wholesome personality, sunny disposition and determination, together with her ability and high ideals, assure for her a prosperous and happy future.

name: McKennahan, Cleon Claude
description: "Non" came to us this year from Clarke College. Because of his winning disposition, he has made many friends. No task is ever approached with fear or dread. He has energy that is conserved and wisely directed, making every stroke successful.

name: McCoy, Elmer E.
motto: "Jolly the fellow who's down today— Give him a smile for his sorrow; For the world sometimes has a funny way, And you may be down tomorrow."
clubs: President Everready B. Y. P. U., '29
Associate Director B. Y. P. U., '30

name: Moore, Margaret Isadore
motto: "Friendship consists in forgetting what one gives, and remembering what one receives."
description: To know Margaret is to love her, for she is one of those whose gospel is. We predict success for Margaret, for she is an excellent student whose kindness wins the hearts of all.

name: Vanlandingham, Pauline
clubs: Vice-President and Executive Committee Elementary Council, '30
description: High ideals, attractive personality and a lovable disposition have made Pauline loved by her associates. She is Irish, and very original.

name: Thomas, Harry M.
description: Harry is one who thinks deeply and feels keenly. He is artistic, intellectual, original and intensely human.

name: Robinson, Sara Louise
description: Vivacious, full of life and pep, and never too tired to lead any undertaking that is worth while. She is usually happy and sees the bright side of everything. We admire her for her frankness of opinion.

name: Taylor, Maggie M.
description: Maggie comes with a smile, goes with a smile. Nothing daunts her. She loves life, and finds it full and worth while. She is a perfect combination of charms and—when you say "personality"—well, everybody knows that's Maggie.

name: Whitney, Gladys Pou
description: Gladys, the piquant, pretty girl of our class, is noted for her mental ability and social leadership, as evidenced by the honors she has had bestowed upon her by her fellow students. She is an artist of whom we are proud.

name: Sherrill, Thelma
description: There's language in her eyes, her cheeks, her lips. Grace is in all her steps; dignity and love in every gesture. Thelma to us is sweetness, ability and true friendship. She comes from Florida, and the balmy breezes are reflected in her smiles. Thanks to Florida.

name: Fleming, A. B.
description: "Red" has an eye for business, as has been shown by his thoroughness as business manager of the "Neka Camon." Yet we never hear him grumble or fret—he just does his part like a man without finding fault in things unpleasant, which is a splendid trait in his manly character. "Red" is a leader among men and a winner among women. His wonderful record at S. T. C. convinces us that he has a great future. Luck to you, old boy.

name: Blackwell, Ida
description: Her quiet, unassuming way, high ideals, lovable character and attractive personality have endeared Ida to both faculty and students.

name: Suttle, Myrtle Earle
description: Only by a close and personal friendship can one know the real worth of Myrtle Earle. She is a combination of honesty, ability, efficiency, determination, originality and pep. She leaves well equipped for the Math room.

name: Yow, Zeke
description: Zeke is the type of student college folk love—always smiling, never blue—ever jolly. A man who is a credit to our college. Zeke is not a large man, but if we weigh him for what he is worth—well, we are struck by his heaviness.

name: Chrestman, J. H.
motto: "Not too serious, not too gay, But altogether a jolly good fellow."

name: Melton, Jewel
description: A rare combination of wit, humor, sense and stability, with the quality of looking on the bright side of life, together with the most admirable of traits, that of being a good friend—that's Jewel. Jewel joined our class in our senior year, and she has been an invaluable addition in every way. Here's wishing you, Jewel, the best life can offer.

name: Reynolds, F. J.
description: As for a description of Frank's likableness, words are not sufficient to give it. Friendship is his golden motto, and every day he strives to live it.

name: Gray, Lexie
description: Loveable, capable and always ready to lend a helping hand makes her a favorite with all who know her. She is one who merits the highest esteem, regard and honor. We know of nothing that can turn her from the path of success.

name: Brantley, W. A.
description: "Bulldog" is everything that his name implies—courageous, determined and steadfast. He is always first in work, first in play. To say the least of "Bulldog," he's an all-round good fellow. That's why we're for him.

name: Helen, Oliver
motto: "Take it easy; have your fun, And let the old world flicker on."

name: Cook, Annie Bailey
clubs: Faculty Committee for Field Co-operative Association, '27, '28, '29, '30
description: Mrs. Annie Bailey Cook is a leader and lover of girls, wise in her counsels and firm in her judgments. She holds high rank in class performances. She has had the unique experience of being a popular student and a popular officer at one and the same time. If she should leave S. T. C. when she receives her degree the college would scarcely seem the same to hundreds of girls at home-coming time.

name: Braddock, Mary F.
motto: "None but herself can be herself."

name: Chamblee, Nettye
motto: "Not failure, but low aim is crime."
description: Nettie is one whose aim is high, not only in big things, but in the little as well. She has succeeded in becoming proficient in the art of teaching and in the more difficult art of living.

name: Hitt, Emma Elizabeth
motto: "Her first love is athletics; Her second is mathematics."
description: Speaking of good, all-round college girls, have you seen our "Hitt"? With her sunny disposition, bright smile and ready sympathy, she makes a pal worth having. Along with this add brains, a good athlete and a good, snappy line, and you have our "Hitt" in a nutshell.

name: McIntosh, Irma Lee
motto: "A strong, rich nature, free to trust; Truthful and almost sternly just."
description: Irma, with her charming personality, her fine mind, and her strength of character, should be a winner of life's finest prizes.

name: Scarborough, Opal
motto: "Sparkling laughter, primed with wit, Hundred-eight pounds of capital IT; A headful of knowledge all 'A' grade, One responsible for friends she's made."

name: Phillips, L. G.
motto: "Nor look nor tone revealeth aught Save woman's quietness of thought; And yet around her is a light Of inward majesty and might."

name: Neal, C. B.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Platonian Literary Society, '28
description: C. B. likes a good joke as well as he likes to joke a good friend. He loves the jolly side of life because he is a jolly, good fellow. He doesn't believe life has a worry, for he never worries. Don't you worry—he'll be in there fighting to the end.

name: Haney, Donnis
motto: "A smile when she wins; a smile when she loses."
description: One who never turns her back, but marches breast forward, Donnis makes friends with both students and faculty. She is by nature a girl of high ideals and ambitions. She has few words, but they mean worlds. We doubt not that her name will appear in the future as a success.

name: Worthington, Mary Mason
description: Mary has a "spicy" outlook on life. Due to her major? Eh, what? Anyway, she's often mistaken for a happy-go-lucky freshman. Absolutely she's "Always happy and full of fun; E'er on hand when there's work to be done; Always kind, dependable, true— A very good friend for me and you."

name: Ladner, Olander L.
motto: "No mountain too high, no valley too deep— No path too rugged, no cliff too steep."
description: Ladner works with an iron will, finding life's richest treasures in the tasks set before him. His studious inclinations lead him into the deeps of college life, where the roots of success lie thickest.

name: Thomas, Percy L.
motto: "Woman is man's bread of life, therefore I am always hungry."

name: Walters, Alpha Ola
motto: "Our homes are cheerier for her sake, Our door yards brighter blooming, And all about the social air Is sweeter for her coming."
description: Faithful, capable and kind, Alpha will make an ideal teacher of home economics, or, better still, the ideal homemaker!

name: McWilliams, Lois
description: Everybody thinks of "Mac" as being truly Southern, in her beauty, in her charm, in her sweetness of character, in her purity of soul. She carries herself with an air of grace and refinement, and even more, she's aristocratic. Would we have her other than as she is?

name: Kinard, C. B.
description: Ability, unselfishness, determination, sincerity, with added neatness all combined in one, gives S.T.C. the personage of Kinard. Ever ready to undertake a task and embodied with will power to see that task well done. These character traits are sure to bring him success.

name: Harlan, Hilbun
motto: "Send to us that precious stock the store, With your great wit, and we'll not ask for more."

name: Carter, Eddie
motto: "You must sit down!" says Love, 'and taste my meat'; So I did sit and eat."

name: Burris, Anne Hewit
motto: "For if she will, she will, and you may depend on it; And if she won't, she won't, and there's an end on it"

name: Tatum, Cleo
description: A capable, conscientious student and a constant friend. Cleo has that rare quality, dependability.

name: Gates, Minnie
motto: "The reason firm, the temperate will, Endurance, foresight, strength and skill... To warn, to comfort and command."

name: Mohler, Margaret Spenser
description: Margaret—excellent in scholarship, bubbling over with action and the pure joy of living. A priceless friend.

name: Broadway, Hurshel H.
description: Broadway! New York's part of S. T. C. A lover of chemistry—poetically inclined—greets all with a smile.

name: Wright, Estelle
motto: "Why don't the men propose, mama? Why don't the men propose?"

name: Blocker, Charles C.
description: Endowed with more talent than is common among men—musical ability of Beethoven—oratorical persuasion of Patrick Henry—poetical ability of Homer—that's Charles.

name: Upton, Mary Elizabeth
clubs: President Sherwood Bonner Literary Society, '29, Vice-President, '30
description: Within the shell of dignity, aloofness and "hard-boiled-ness" is encased a rare and precious jewel.

name: Reedy, Carra
description: Carra is just as businesslike as she is domestic. She is loved for her bigness of heart and loyalty to friends.

name: Seymore, Mildred
motto: "She's gay, she's true, she's sad and bad, like me or you; but good or bad, gay or sad, she's just Mildred."

name: Coody, Hazel
motto: "For if she will, she will, and you may depend on it; And if she won't, she won't, and there's an end on it."

name: Wilson, Guy T.
motto: "He climbed stately trees by the impulses of ambitious sprees; Now he chiefly delves in themes of literature, preferring the romantic to the pure."

name: Reed, Mrs. Victor
motto: "We love her for her smile, her look, her way Of speaking gently—for a trick of thought That falls in well with ours."
clubs: R. Y. P. U. President Pennybaker Union, '30

name: Womack, Mildred
motto: "When I don't know whether to fight or not, I always fight."

name: Bingham, Gladys
description: Bing! Bing! Bingham! A fair example of "why gentlemen prefer blondes." What more could one say?

name: Breeland, Ina Florence
motto: "One like her is seldom met; She does her work without a fret."

name: Mathews, Willie Belle
description: "Billie"—we adore her because of her physical charm, her rare excellence of character, and her purity of mind.

name: Deloache, Mary Elizabeth
description: A better sport—a better friend than Mary Liz—would be hard to find.

name: Sullivan, Agnes
motto: "Did ever any lady weep, Being cheated of her golden sleep, Stol'n by sick thoughts?"

name: Breeland, Corinne
motto: "Be good, sweet maid, and let who will be clever."
description: Corinne was not the girl to whom the poet penned the above line, but she seems to have adopted it as her motto. More and more, however, she is proving that one may be clever as well as good.

name: Mabry, Willa
motto: "A little home just for two, Filled with happiness through and through."
description: Placid as a quiet lake under June skies is Willa—gentle, calm and serene. Here is a perennial happiness that won't wear off.

name: Phillips, H. W.
clubs: Platonician Literary Society, Chaplain Fall Quarter, '30
President Ever-Ready B. Y. P. U., '30
description: As for industry, there is none greater; As for lofty ideals, there is none above him; Just know Phillips and then you love him.

name: Godwin, Osia
description: Cultured, refined and "unk"-that's our Osia.

name: Johnson, Beatrice
motto: "She needs no eulogy. She speaks for herself, for she knows she knows she knows."

name: Godbold, Lottie
motto: "Better to not be at all than not to be noble."

name: Mccleskey, Martha Ann
motto: "A daughter fair, so buxom, blithe and debonair; Come and trip it as you go on the light, fantastic toe."

name: Worthington, Amelie
motto: "A student quite reserved, who helps to make college life idealistic; she takes life and books seriously—but optimistically."

name: Baker, Vera
motto: "And presently her agate eyes take a soft, large, milky haze, And her independent casual glance becomes a stiff, hard gaze."

name: Reed, Victor
description: Mr. Reed's ability to co-ordinate a becoming reserve and dignity has won the respect and confidence of faculty and students.

name: Frank, McLendon
motto: "Though a lover of the ladies, In love he's never been; He's a salesman and a dancer And can shake a wicked shin."

name: Pippins, Fannie Corinne
motto: "Happy am I, from care I'm free; Why ain't they all contented like me?"
clubs: Bible Reader's Leader in B. Y. P. U.

name: East, Jewel
motto: "She's little but she's wise; She's a terror for her size."

name: Petty, Mildred Louise
motto: "Her eyes, her face, her every action bespeaks a neverceasing interest in living."

name: Jackson, Thomas A.
motto: "He holds no parley with unmanly fears, Where duty bids, he confidently steers."

name: Ashley, Ruby Cathryn
motto: "A smile for all, a greeting glad, A lovable, jolly way she had."
clubs: Poet and Cheer Leader Sophomore Class, '29

name: Moore, Martha Frances
motto: "Doubt does not accomplish things; belief accomplishes."

name: Megehee, Louis
motto: "He puts a boot behind the ball; He'll make a mark in Fame's own Hall."
